question: 1. Which of the following sets of numbers forms a balanced heap after the insert heap step is performed?

explanations.a: Incorrect. The sequence 1, 4, 3, 9, 2, 8, 6, 7 does not maintain the heap property after insertion, as not all parent nodes are less than or equal to their children in a min-heap.
explanations.b: Correct. 2, 3, 9, 1, 6, 7, 4 can be arranged to form a balanced heap after the insert operation, maintaining the heap property.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Only one of the given sets forms a balanced heap after insertion, not both.
explanations.d: Incorrect. There is enough information to determine which set forms a balanced heap.

question: 2. What do you think is the complexity of inserting into a heap using this naive insert heap method? (Consider if it can be further improved)

explanations.a: Correct. The naive method of inserting into a heap has a time complexity of O(n) because each insertion may require shifting all elements.
explanations.b: Incorrect. O(log n) is the complexity for the optimal heap insertion, not the naive method.
explanations.c: Incorrect. O(n log n) is the complexity for building a heap using repeated optimal insertions, not the naive method described here.
explanations.d: Incorrect. O(n^2) is much higher than the actual complexity for this operation.
